title: Living in Boulder vlog 9-16-2018: History Books, still

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives an update on his ongoing history books website project, which he's been stuck on for weeks working through ancient texts like the Vedas and Augustine. He discusses his philosophy around speed reading, website design for dense information, and his cycle of getting stressed and wanting to exercise but using the website as justification not to. He also outlines roughly 2.5 years of planned website projects including Google Earth-style maps of Rome and Boulder, and floats a Patreon idea where he'd only start exercising if fans buy him a time trial bike.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: The world needs all history books compiled in one uniform browsable format — this is more valuable than modern commentary
Maximize text density on screen to enable speed reading; children's-book-style big-font paginated apps are the wrong direction
People should anchor around original ancient texts like Greek philosophers rather than following modern commentators like Sam Harris or Ben Shapiro
Knows it would help his learning and mental health but is using the website as justification to delay indefinitely
Sees merit in building his website solo, and plans to leverage not being paid by Google as a point of principle
Willing to join Patreon but only to crowdfund a time trial bike as a condition for starting exercise — framed as a psychological hack

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This is my third time doing the video because the first time the microphone volume was too low.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The second time I recorded the video, I was naked and the camera angle revealed too much.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have been working on history books for my website, specifically stuck on the Vedas and Rig Vedas.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to create a new template for the Vedas because the previous method was inefficient.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Augustine's works took a long time to process due to the numerous chapters requiring pagination.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I am currently working on St. John Chrysostom's texts, which are also extensive.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I estimate it will take at least another week to finish polishing the history books on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have been working on this history book project for an exceptionally long time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I am experiencing stress and an identity crisis, possibly due to smoking weed frequently.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I considered starting exercising to alleviate stress but haven't followed through.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to join Patreon but have been delaying because I need to make a three-minute video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I joked that if people buy me a high-end time trial bike, I would start exercising.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe having a time trial bike would allow me to conduct proper scientific experiments on biking.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I often cycle between working on my website and getting distracted by exercise equipment purchases.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think Patreon could help me focus on my website by providing a financial incentive.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I explained to my mom that the way people read books has evolved into a more interactive, scan-based method.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I advocate for reading foundational texts repeatedly rather than modern, superficial books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe ancient Greek and Christian texts serve as a cultural constitution that grounds society.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have processed over 121,000 pages of books on my website, scanning them multiple times.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I use speed reading techniques like scanning for numbers, quotes, and specific keywords.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to implement color-coding in my books to enhance speed reading efficiency.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I designed my website to maximize text visibility, contrasting with modern apps that mimic children's books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I criticized modern websites for not utilizing screen space efficiently for speed reading.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to integrate Google Earth and stock photos into my history books for better visualization.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I fear wasting time if someone else completes a project I started, like Boulder Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I estimated it would take two and a half more years to complete all my website projects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I considered switching from bike racing to triathlons but haven't committed yet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I joked about using a 'bicycle hostage technique' to motivate myself to finish my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I never participated in athletics as a kid and now feel the need to experience it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe physical activity enhances mental learning and creativity.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I noticed an increase in views on my last video, possibly due to interest in my training plans.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I compared my Patreon strategy to Donald Trump's tactic of saying outrageous things to gauge public reaction.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I admitted to neglecting basic chores like cleaning my bathroom for months due to focusing on my website.
